response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese (candidate speaks English, Romanian, and German), deep C/UNIX embedded development, and Linux kernel source-modification experience — the candidate's career centers on web and full-stack development in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments a decade ago. There is no evidence of networking security, MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware experience.
strengths: Extensive, well-rounded senior software engineering experience (15+ years) with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Solid Linux background and some low-level/systems exposure (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, hardware work with robotic arms)
Demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly and work across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement for the role
Lacks required embedded networking/security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, Linux kernel modification)
C/UNIX experience is dated and brief; recent work is almost entirely web/full-stack TypeScript

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and Linux familiarity
